sutta: is another very good suit buddha said in bygone days monks there arose a teacher named sunito a core setter freed of all lustful passions now he had many hundreds of disciples to whom he taught the doctrine of fellowship in brahma's world and those who inclined not their hearts towards the teacher's doctrine on the breaking up of the body after death arose in the untoward way the evil way the abyss hell but those who incline their hearts to his doctrine on the breaking up of the body after death arose in heaven that happy plays are faring on in bygone days monks there were teachers named and each of them taught similar doctrines and their disciples fared according to the inclination of their hearts now what thank you monks for an evil-minded man to revile and defame these 7 teachers of all core setters freed from all lustful passions surrounded by many hundreds of disciples would he be greatly married yes lord indeed monks he would but the evil-minded man who reviles and defames a person of right view he for that begets much more demerit and wise then i declare monks he dicks not so great a fit for himself in reviling outsiders as in reviling his fellows in the holy life therefore monks train yourselves thus not unto us shall there come evil thoughts about our fellows in the holy life thus must you train yourselves monks as the end of the sutta
commentary: so in this this buddha is talking about various external aesthetics in the past and he calls them course that is called setters course sector is 1 who starts a new segment who starts a course a new teaching starts a new segment a new religion and the buddha also calls them describes them as being free of all lustful passions the reason is because they have attained jhanas because they teach doctrine of fellowship in brahma's world that means they teach the brahma-viharas meditations on loving-kindness meta mudita joy and karuna compassion and upeka equanimity and when a person cultivates his brahma viharas that person attains the jaanas and because of that the buddha describes as them as being freed of all lustful passions because when the person attains the jaanas according to the sutasa the uh 5 hindrances are abandoned abandoned means they are cut down just like tall grass it's cut down it doesn't impede you anymore it doesn't hinder you anymore but that doesn't mean the roots are uprooted the roots are still there but because they are cut down they normal hinder a person so that's why they are described as being freed of all lustful passions now these 7 core sectors are supposed to be the previous rebirths of the bodhisattva or buddha in the previous life and here the buddha is saying that you if you revile such teachers who attain jhana and who teach a doctrine or fellowship in brahma's world then you get much very much d married and so in reviling a person who attains jhana you attain you get much d married because jhana is described in the vinaya as 1 of the what they call uttari manosa dhamma dhamma which is higher than a human attainment higher than a human attainment it's not a normal human state that's why some people they don't understand the jhanas they say in a state of ghana there is no mindfulness it's absolutely not true state of jana is a state of intense mindfulness and it's very difficult to attain a state of jana just like it's difficult to become an arya it's not not not not something that a normal person easily gets that's why it's called a uttari manosa dhamma so just reviling a person who attains jhana you get much d married but if you revile a person a defame a person with right view that means a person who understands the dhamma then you get very much more d married and the buddha said i declare monks he dicks not so great a pit for himself in reviling outsiders as in reviling his fellows in the holy life that means you revile you uh abuse somebody and external sex ascetic you get must be married but you abuse a manga in the buddhist religion then you get much much more demerit now if that person understands the dhamma and has right riola so this is a warning that we should be careful not to have hatred for amongst uh especially if they keep the vinaya and they meditate they cultivate themselves especially even if you have any difference of opinion with them not to have any hatred for them the next utah
